title: How to Install Software on Linux
description: Learn the different ways to install programs on Linux — from package managers and software centers to Flatpak, Snap, and AppImage.
prerequisites: A Linux distribution installed (Ubuntu, Debian, Mint, etc.)

steps.title: Use the Software Center (GUI)
steps.content: Most Linux distributions come with a graphical software center, similar to the App Store or Microsoft Store. On Ubuntu it's called **Ubuntu Software**, on Linux Mint it's the **Software Manager**, and on Pop!_OS it's the **Pop!_Shop**. Open it, search for the app you want, and click Install. It's the easiest way to get started.

steps.title: Install with APT (Debian/Ubuntu)
steps.content: APT is the default package manager on Debian-based distributions like Ubuntu, Linux Mint, and Pop!_OS. It downloads software from official repositories — curated collections of tested, trusted packages.
steps.code:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install firefox vlc gimp

steps.title: Search for available packages
steps.content: Not sure of the exact package name? Use the search command to find it. This searches all available repositories for matching packages.
steps.code:
apt search video-editor
apt show kdenlive

steps.title: Remove software
steps.content: To uninstall a program, use the `remove` command. Add `--purge` to also delete its configuration files. Then run `autoremove` to clean up unused dependencies.
steps.code:
sudo apt remove firefox
sudo apt autoremove

steps.title: Install Flatpak apps
steps.content: Flatpak is a universal package format that works across all distributions. Apps run in a sandbox for extra security and always get the latest version. Flathub is the main Flatpak repository with thousands of apps.
steps.code:
sudo apt install flatpak
flatpak remote-add --if-not-exists flathub https://flathub.org/repo/flathub.flatpakrepo
flatpak install flathub com.spotify.Client

steps.title: Install Snap packages
steps.content: Snap is another universal package format, developed by Canonical. It comes pre-installed on Ubuntu. Snaps update automatically and also run sandboxed.
steps.code:
sudo snap install code --classic
snap list

steps.title: Run an AppImage
steps.content: AppImages are standalone executables — no installation needed. Download the `.AppImage` file, make it executable, and run it. It's like a portable app.

steps.title: Install from .deb files
steps.content: Some developers provide `.deb` packages for download (like Google Chrome or Discord). You can install these directly. This is similar to running an `.exe` installer on Windows.
steps.warning: Only download .deb files from official sources. Unlike repository packages, .deb files are not reviewed by your distribution.

seo.title: How to Install Software on Linux — APT, Flatpak, Snap & More
seo.description: Complete beginner guide to installing programs on Linux. Learn to use APT, Flatpak, Snap, AppImage, and .deb files on Ubuntu, Debian, and Mint.
keywords: how to install programs on linux
